english: KFC spicy crispy chicken burger Singapore

region_of_origin: USA (KFC global product); served in Singapore
food_subgroup: Fast food spicy crispy chicken breast fillet burger with coleslaw and spicy sauce

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn URI exists specifically for KFC Zinger Burger; closest parent 'chicken sandwich' (FOODON_03315507) used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No exact FoodEx2 code for KFC Zinger; A0GBY (fast food chicken burger) used as closest parent.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No applicable SNOMED CT code for this commercial fast food product.

serving.natural_language_portion: One KFC Zinger Burger weighing approximately 180g, comprising a deep-fried spicy crispy chicken breast fillet, coleslaw, spicy sauce, and a white sesame bun.

nutrient_source.title: KFC Singapore Nutrition Information — Zinger Burger
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutritional values sourced directly from KFC Singapore official nutrition page for the Zinger Burger. Per-burger values (430 kcal, 22g protein, 16g fat, 4g saturated fat, 45g carbohydrate, 910mg sodium) confirmed from official data and back-calculated to per-100g basis using 180g serving weight. Micronutrient values (potassium, calcium, magnesium, iron, phosphorus, zinc, selenium, vitamins) are estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for comparable deep-fried breaded chicken breast sandwich components where KFC Singapore does not publish full micronutrient panels.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — white bread and refined wheat bun analogues
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. Diabetes Care 2008
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for the KFC Zinger Burger. GI ~65 is an analogous estimate based on the primary carbohydrate source being a white sesame bun (~35g refined wheat carbohydrate) with GI ~70–75 for white bread/bun, moderated downward to ~65 by the presence of protein (22g), fat (16g), and coleslaw fibre, consistent with mixed fast food meal GI estimates in the University of Sydney GI Database. GL = (GI × available carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = (65 × 45) / 100 = 29.25, rounded to 29.3.

hypertension.key_factor: 910mg sodium per burger (505.6mg per 100g) — approximately 45.5% of the 2,000mg daily sodium limit in a single meal item
hypertension.guidance: Hypertensive patients should treat the KFC Zinger Burger as an occasional indulgence only; request no extra Zinger sauce (saves ~40mg sodium) and avoid pairing with other high-sodium sides such as fries or gravy to keep the total meal sodium below 1,200mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: GI ~65 (medium), GL ~29.3 per burger — moderate-to-high glycaemic impact from the refined white sesame bun (~35g available carbohydrate from bun alone)
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should limit the KFC Zinger Burger to occasional consumption; the relatively lower fat content (16g vs 27g in comparable thigh-meat burgers) means less fat-mediated slowing of gastric emptying, so postprandial glucose may rise more sharply than the GL alone suggests — pairing with a non-starchy vegetable side and avoiding sugary beverages is advised.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High sodium (910mg), moderate saturated fat (4g per burger, 2.2g per 100g), and deep-frying-derived oxidised lipid products (advanced lipoxidation end-products) present in the fried coating
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with cardiovascular disease should limit consumption to no more than once per fortnight; the breast-meat formulation keeps saturated fat lower than dark-meat fried chicken alternatives, but the high sodium load and ultra-processed nature of the product remain significant cardiovascular risk factors.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High sodium (910mg) relevant for patients on corticosteroids; deep-frying produces pro-inflammatory advanced lipoxidation end-products (ALEs), though at lower levels than dark-meat fried chicken due to the lower fat content of breast meat
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: RA patients on corticosteroids should limit sodium intake and treat this burger as an occasional item; the breast-meat formulation produces fewer oxidised fat by-products than thigh-meat alternatives, but the deep-fried coating still contributes pro-inflammatory compounds.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The KFC Zinger Burger (Singapore market) is a deep-fried spicy crispy chicken breast fillet burger served in a white sesame bun with coleslaw and spicy sauce, weighing approximately 180g per serving. It delivers approximately 430 kcal, 22g protein, 16g fat (4g saturated), 45g carbohydrate, and 910mg sodium per burger. The Zinger is a globally recognised KFC signature product available in over 100 countries and certified halal in Singapore. A key clinical distinction from McDonald's McSpicy is that the Zinger uses BREAST MEAT (leaner, lower fat) while the McSpicy uses THIGH MEAT (higher fat, juicier), making the Zinger lower in calories (430 vs 555 kcal) and total fat (16g vs 27g) while remaining similarly high in sodium (910 vs 1,040mg). The GI is estimated at ~65 (medium), driven primarily by the refined white sesame bun, with a glycaemic load of ~29.3 per burger — a moderate-to-high glycaemic impact. The deep-fried coating contributes oxidised lipid by-products (ALEs) that are pro-inflammatory, though at lower levels than dark-meat fried chicken. Clinically, the Zinger is unsuitable for hypertensive patients as a regular meal item due to its high sodium content, and should be consumed only occasionally by people with Type 2 Diabetes or cardiovascular disease. Micronutrient highlights include niacin (4.5mg/100g), phosphorus (140mg/100g), and potassium (200mg/100g) from the chicken breast component.

confidence_reason: Core macronutrient values (energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate, sodium) sourced directly from KFC Singapore official nutrition data. Per-100g values back-calculated from verified per-burger values using the stated 180g serving weight. Micronutrient values estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy for comparable deep-fried breaded chicken breast sandwich components where KFC Singapore does not publish full micronutrient panels; these are marked with source notes accordingly. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database methodology.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown not published by KFC Singapore and not available for this specific product formulation in USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods as of April 2026. water_g set to -1: moisture content not reported by KFC Singapore and not determinable from available approved sources without laboratory analysis. trans_fat_g is 0.0 (confirmed zero per KFC Singapore official nutrition data). Micronutrient values (potassium, calcium, magnesium, iron, phosphorus, zinc, selenium, vitamins) are estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (USDA FDC ID 171477 — Chicken, broilers or fryers, breast, meat only, cooked, fried — and composite bun/coleslaw components) where KFC Singapore does not publish full micronutrient panels; confidence on these micronutrient values is medium. GI of 65 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for KFC Zinger Burger exists in the University of Sydney GI Database as of April 2026. The food_subgroup in the input contained truncated text; this has been normalised. country_of_origin and region fields in the input contained truncated text; these have been normalised to 'USA (KFC global product); served in Singapore'.
